title: Ingest GeoTIFFs as Predictors
text: , ’ll start relatively simple predictor: current level noise pollution, accordance current levels traffic.
code: coords_minimal <- db$coords_minimal  sonbase_spec <- list(   noise = list(     unit = "dBa",     pretty_name = "Maximum noise exposure",     orig_format = "10m*10m raster",     description = "The maximum across all sonBASE noise exposure categories, i.e. daytime & nighttime road & rail exposure",     sources = list(       list(         url = "https://data.geo.admin.ch/ch.bafu.laerm-strassenlaerm_tag/laerm-strassenlaerm_tag/laerm-strassenlaerm_tag_2056.tif",         md5sum = "09791808dbf12fcde82182e70f2ebdfb"       ),       list(         url = "https://data.geo.admin.ch/ch.bafu.laerm-strassenlaerm_nacht/laerm-strassenlaerm_nacht/laerm-strassenlaerm_nacht_2056.tif",         md5sum = "6e79dc1d353751084e21dc6b61778b99"       )       # list(       #   url = "https://data.geo.admin.ch/ch.bafu.laerm-bahnlaerm_nacht/laerm-bahnlaerm_nacht/laerm-bahnlaerm_nacht_2056.tif",       #   md5sum = "161df62f9a2a29c9120380f965aa19ba"       # ),       # list(       #   url = "https://data.geo.admin.ch/ch.bafu.laerm-bahnlaerm_tag/laerm-bahnlaerm_tag/laerm-bahnlaerm_tag_2056.tif",       #   md5sum = "6016b9ca4c974cb982fbe18112f201fe"       # )     )   ) )  sonbase_sources <-   sonbase_spec$noise$sources |>   data.table::rbindlist() |>   download_and_verify()  # data is at 10m # extent plus 1km, given metres for unit; enough for all resampling strategies extent_wide <- db$extent |> terra::extend(1000)  sonbase_max <-   lapply(     sonbase_sources$local_path,     \(x) terra::rast(x) |> terra::crop(extent_wide)   ) |>   terra::rast() |>   max(na.rm = TRUE) |>   terra::resample(10, method = "bilinear") |> # downsample factor 10: to hectare   extract_using_coords_t(coords_minimal)  db$add_predictor(   pred_spec = sonbase_spec,   pred_data = sonbase_max[     ,     # the id_period = 0L is a 0 integer; this special period indicates "static" data     # that's not associated with a historical period     .(id_coord, id_period = 0L, value)   ],   pred_type = "float" )

title: Installing Dinamica EGO
text: Warning Dinamica EGO distributed Linux x86 / amd64 AppImage Windows installer. run ARM architectures (e.g. Apple Silicon, Raspberry Pi) macOS. Linux AppImage path described vignette well-tested; Windows installer tested evoland-plus. unsupported platform, best option build Docker image https://github.com/ethzplus/rocker-geospatial-dinamica/. Dinamica EGO redistributed, image pre-built; must clone repository build locally. evoland-plus repository ships devcontainer configuration, image built can open project directly inside container VS Code IDE supports devcontainer specification — just make sure Docker-compatible runtime running first (e.g. Docker Desktop, OrbStack, Colima, Portainer).

title: Installing Dinamica EGO
text: steps mirror Dockerfile used build official container image. Download Dinamica EGO 8 https://dinamicaego.com/dinamica-8/ save DinamicaEGO.AppImage. AppImages self-contained archives. Run following extract Dinamica’s contents squashfs-root subdirectory: Move contents extracted squashfs-root directory (directory ) /opt/dinamica: Dinamica ships R package inside installation directory. Using remotes::install_local() recommended automatically resolves installs package’s dependencies (listed DESCRIPTION file). alternative — R CMD INSTALL — require find install dependency hand first. Add following lines shell profile (e.g. ~/.bashrc, ~/.profile, ~/.zshenv Zsh users) set every session, reload profile log back : Note LD_LIBRARY_PATH need set manually. evoland automatically prepends /opt/dinamica/usr/lib (derived DINAMICA_EGO_8_HOME) LD_LIBRARY_PATH invokes DinamicaConsole, system-wide library path never affected. Create temporary directory Dinamica uses runtime: Dinamica looks per-user configuration file home directory. Create ~/.dinamica_ego_8.conf least following content. AlternativePathForR key must point Rscript binary evoland uses; verify correct path Rscript terminal update value accordingly. Adjust CRAN mirror prefer different one: Check Dinamica can found PATH: see version information printed terminal. R, run Dinamica-specific test file ships evoland confirm full integration works: Alternatively, run full test suite (takes longer validates complete installation):
code: chmod +x DinamicaEGO.AppImage ./DinamicaEGO.AppImage --appimage-extract sudo mkdir -p /opt/dinamica sudo mv squashfs-root/* /opt/dinamica/ Rscript -e "remotes::install_local(   list.files(     '/opt/dinamica/usr/bin/Data/R',     pattern = 'Dinamica_.*.tar.gz',     recursive = TRUE,     full.names = TRUE   ) )" export DINAMICA_EGO_8_INSTALLATION_DIRECTORY="/opt/dinamica/usr/bin" export DINAMICA_EGO_CLI="/opt/dinamica/usr/bin/DinamicaConsole" export DINAMICA_EGO_8_HOME="/opt/dinamica" export DINAMICA_EGO_8_TEMP_DIR="/tmp/dinamica" export PATH="$PATH:/opt/dinamica/usr/bin" mkdir -p /tmp/dinamica AlternativePathForR = "/usr/local/bin/Rscript" ClConfig = "0" MemoryAllocationPolicy = "1" RCranMirror = "https://cloud.r-project.org/" DinamicaConsole --version library(evoland) tinytest::run_test_file(   system.file("tinytest", "test_util_dinamica.R", package = "evoland") ) tinytest::test_package("evoland")

text: Dinamica ships R package inside installation directory. Using remotes::install_local() recommended automatically resolves installs package’s dependencies (listed DESCRIPTION file). alternative — R CMD INSTALL — require find install dependency hand first.
code: Rscript -e "remotes::install_local(   list.files(     '/opt/dinamica/usr/bin/Data/R',     pattern = 'Dinamica_.*.tar.gz',     recursive = TRUE,     full.names = TRUE   ) )"

title: Installing Dinamica EGO
text: Add following lines shell profile (e.g. ~/.bashrc, ~/.profile, ~/.zshenv Zsh users) set every session, reload profile log back : Note LD_LIBRARY_PATH need set manually. evoland automatically prepends /opt/dinamica/usr/lib (derived DINAMICA_EGO_8_HOME) LD_LIBRARY_PATH invokes DinamicaConsole, system-wide library path never affected. Create temporary directory Dinamica uses runtime:
code: export DINAMICA_EGO_8_INSTALLATION_DIRECTORY="/opt/dinamica/usr/bin" export DINAMICA_EGO_CLI="/opt/dinamica/usr/bin/DinamicaConsole" export DINAMICA_EGO_8_HOME="/opt/dinamica" export DINAMICA_EGO_8_TEMP_DIR="/tmp/dinamica" export PATH="$PATH:/opt/dinamica/usr/bin" mkdir -p /tmp/dinamica

title: evoland-plus - or evoland for short - analyses land use evolution
text: evoland-plus R package (evoland short) provides tools analyzing projecting land use evolution. package implements statistically calibrated, constrained model predicting locations future land use / land cover change (LULCC). fundamental purpose evoland-plus : Gather process land use/land cover data multiple sources clearly defined database structure. Calibrate statistical models understand historical land use transitions Project future land use patterns using predictive models allocation algorithms Support scenario analysis interventions parameter modifications

title: evoland-plus - or evoland for short - analyses land use evolution
text: suggest use rv manage dependencies encapsulated environment. Simply create rproject.toml project root execute rv sync. want develop model logic , can opt install dependencies package : development, devtools::load_all() acts though calling library(evoland) installed package. makes easy rapidly reload code ’ve changed something.
code: # sample rproject.toml [project] name = "evoland-plus" r_version = "4.5"  repositories = [     { alias = "CRAN", url = "https://stat.ethz.ch/CRAN/" }, ] dependencies = [     # just use it: load using library(evoland)     { name = "evoland", git = "https://github.com/ethzplus/evoland-plus/", branch = "main" }, ] dependencies = [         # only install dependencies declared in DESCRIPTION     { name = "evoland", path = "~/path/to/local/repo", dependencies_only = true, install_suggestions = true },     "devtools", # sundry development tasks     "mirai", # modern multiprocessing     "httpgd", # webview plotting     "languageserver", # assuming your IDE loads the rv library instead of system ]

title: Compute fuzzy neighborhood similarity between two categorical rasters — calc_fuzzy_similarity
text: fuzzy similarity based Hagen (2003) uses concept fuzziness location. cell, fuzzy neighborhood vector computed represents membership category within specified window, weighted distance. similarity two maps computed intersection fuzzy vectors. exponential decay function : exp(-d / decay_divisor) d Euclidean distance center cell.

title: Two stage covariate filtering — covariance_filter
text: covariance_filter returns set covariates land use land cover change (LULCC) models based two-stage variable selection: first statistical fit estimates covariate's quality given prediction task. second step selects variables given correlation threshold: iterate correlation matrix ordered first step. Starting within leftmost column, rows (.e. candidates) greater given threshold dropped full set candidates. candidate selection retained used select next column, columns left investigate. columns iterated returned character vector selected variable names.

Defaults polynomial GLM p-value ranking. weights Optional weights vector corcut Correlation cutoff threshold ... Additional arguments passed rank_fun. x numeric vector representing single covariate y binary outcome vector (0/1) trans_result Binary outcome vector (0/1) legacy Bool, use legacy weighting? cor_mat Absolute correlation matrix

text: set column names (covariates) retain

title: Two stage covariate filtering — covariance_filter
text: function first ranks covariates using provided ranking function (default: quasibinomial polynomial GLM). , iteratively removes highly (Pearson) correlated variables based correlation cutoff threshold, preserving variables order ranking. See https://github.com/ethzplus/evoland-plus-legacy/blob/main/R/lulcc.covfilter.r concept came . original author Antoine Adde, edits Benjamin Black. similar mechanism found https://github.com/antadde/covsel/.

title: Two stage covariate filtering — covariance_filter
text: rank_poly_glm(): Default ranking function using polynomial GLM. Returns lower p value polynomial terms compute_balanced_weights(): Compute class-balanced weights imbalanced binary outcomes; returns numeric vector select_by_correlation(): Implements iterative selection procedure.

what: Guided Regularized Random Forest Feature Selection — grrf_filter
title: Guided Regularized Random Forest Feature Selection — grrf_filter
text: grrf_filter returns set covariates land use land cover change (LULCC) models based feature selection Guided Regularized Random Forests. two-stage random forest approach: first unregularized random forest estimates variable importance scores. scores used guide second regularized random forest penalizes less important features, resulting parsimonious feature set.

title: Guided Regularized Random Forest Feature Selection — grrf_filter
code: grrf_filter(   data,   weights = compute_balanced_weights(data[["did_transition"]]),   gamma = 0.5,   num.trees = 500,   max.depth = 100,   ... )

title: Guided Regularized Random Forest Feature Selection — grrf_filter
text: data data.table target variable candidate covariates filtered; wide format one predictor per column. Target variable expected named "did_transition". weights Optional named vector class weights. NULL, class-balanced weights computed automatically using compute_grrf_weights(). gamma Numeric 0-1 controlling weight normalized importance score ("importance coefficient"). gamma = 0, perform unguided regularized random forest (guiding effect). gamma = 1, apply strongest guiding effect, leading penalization redundant features concise feature sets. Default 0.5. num.trees Number trees grow random forest. Default 500. ... Additional arguments passed ranger::ranger().

title: Guided Regularized Random Forest Feature Selection — grrf_filter
text: character vector column names (covariates) retain, ordered importance (important first)

title: Guided Regularized Random Forest Feature Selection — grrf_filter
text: Guided Regularized Random Forest (GRRF) algorithm works follows: Fit initial unregularized random forest obtain variable importance scores Normalize importance scores use compute regularization coefficients: coefReg = (1 - gamma) + gamma * normalized_importance Fit regularized random forest using coefficients penalize splits less important variables Return variables positive importance regularized model Class weights used handle class imbalance. Variables terminal nodes weighted class, splits evaluated using weighted Gini impurity. ranger implementation uses split.select.weights parameter apply regularization penalties, approximating RRF regularization approach.

title: Guided Regularized Random Forest Feature Selection — grrf_filter
text: Deng, H., & Runger, G. (2013). Gene selection guided regularized random forest. Pattern Recognition, 46(12), 3483-3489. https://arxiv.org/pdf/1306.0237.pdf Original implementation Antoine Adde, edited Ben Black adapted ranger evoland-plus team.

title: Parquet database utility functions — parquet_db_utils
text: x data committed. class_name Optional class name prepend "parquet_db_t". Used create specific subclasses. key_cols Optional character vector. Used data.table's data.table::setkey used parquet_db determine columns use upsert operations. Together alternate_key_cols, used define uniqueness constraints. alternate_key_cols Optional character vector. used conjunction key_cols define uniqueness constraints, used upsert operations. Example: numeric primary key whose distinctness identical character primary key column, upsert operations based character key, leaving character<->numeric mapping intact. map_cols Optional character vector. Used coerce columns DuckDB's MAP type, used store named unnested lists R. partition_cols Optional character vector. Used specify columns hive style parquet file partitioning. metadata metadata committed, returned parquet_db $get_table_metadata() attr column function resolve, e.g. "alternate_key_cols", "map_cols", "key_cols". cols columns select. fn function apply element list columns table_name name table bind . mode mode binding, determines behavior committing data. Options : "write_once" (default, allows writing table exist), "upsert" fun underlying function bind R6 method, must self argument with_private Whether also pass R6 reference private argument with_super Whether also pass R6 reference super argument

title: Parquet database utility functions — parquet_db_utils
text: as_parquet_db_t(): Coerce parquet_db_t subclass. coerces object data.table adds attributes parquet_db relies database-like operations. See paramaters details. resolve_cols(): Resolves columns use key, map, partition, etc.; pre-existing schema takes precedence. resolve_partition_clause(): Compose partitioning clause. resolve_metadata_clause(): Compose metadata clause, includes new metadata data's attributes, well existing metadata database; existing metadata safely overwritten. New metadata takes precedence existing metadata commit. atomic vector coerced quoted comma separated list values, recovered parquet_db $get_table_metadata(). Non-atomic metadata values dropped warning. convert_list_cols(): Convert list columns applying fn list_to_kv_df(): Convert named list data.frame "key" "value" columns. kv_df_to_list(): Convert data.frame "key" "value" columns named list. create_table_binding(): Create binding function table, can used fetch commit data table. active binding either returns table (missing argument), upserts (assignment operation) create_method_binding(): Helper function bind function method R6 generator. Simply passes R6 method's arguments -underlying "pure" function, passing R6 reference self, private. cols_to_select_expr(): Paste vector escaped column names SQL select statement, optional table name prefix.
